pageDescription: Discover my reasoning when adding React functionality to CodeFolio
content: [{"id":"fc117dac-769e-426e-bc23-c2c1a98c8f1b","component":"Text","data":{"content":"For the UI layer, React was the obvious choice. It has strong adoption among developers, and I was already comfortable working with it. I also planned to implement a canvas-style page builder similar to SocialStack, which makes page scaffolding extremely straightforward. The long term goal was to support a drag-and-drop editor, so choosing a component-based framework aligned well with that direction.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"a7225c65-ef48-4291-9f1a-88ded51aaf5e","component":"Text","data":{"content":"Supporting this workflow required a fast and flexible build system, so I chose ESBuild. Performance was a major factor, but just as important was simplifying the developer experience. My goal was to make creating plugins, components, or themes as frictionless as possible, allowing developers to extend the system with minimal setup.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"5bc2e892-0ada-4b97-8fcf-20b6fe79a494","component":"Text","data":{"content":"Once this infrastructure was in place, the next step was implementing the page system. This began with the WebSocket component, which integrates with the development WebSocket server. It listens for reload events and triggers a refresh when they are received. It also monitors the connection state and displays a message if the WebSocket disconnects, providing immediate feedback during development.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"15588915-5993-41c5-a2d4-926930a1a455","component":"Text","data":{"content":"Inside this component sits a configuration provider that exposes a hook called useModuleConfig. This hook allows components to access module configuration at runtime in a consistent way. The configuration is stored as JSON within the build directory and loaded by the application when needed.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"301050e8-aca5-4a1f-85fb-86d3fdf7c92e","component":"Text","data":{"content":"Configuration itself is managed through the administrative UI. The admin interface provides controls for modifying module settings, and these changes are persisted to the JSON configuration file that the frontend reads at runtime. By exposing this data through the provider, components can retrieve their configuration through useModuleConfig without needing to know where or how the configuration is stored.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"f303e218-c5c9-40f3-a033-a8eeb3fc31a9","component":"Text","data":{"content":"The final piece was the router component. It reads the current pathname from the browser and resolves it to a JSON page definition stored in the content directory. The router constructs the path dynamically based on the requested URL.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"f37e3864-eeb4-425b-ba93-33719f38085b","component":"Text","data":{"content":"For example, if the current pathname is /page/254, the router attempts to load the corresponding file from /content/page/254.json. If the file exists, the router loads the page definition and renders the associated components. If the JSON file cannot be found or fails to load, the router falls back to rendering a 404 page.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]},{"id":"6de1f6c4-e827-4156-b174-f85a92412a9c","component":"Text","data":{"content":"This approach allows routes to be defined purely through content files, keeping the routing layer simple while enabling pages to be managed through the content system.","tag":"p","align":"left","weight":"regular","color":"","fontSize":"","fontFamily":"Inter, sans-serif","lineHeight":"","letterSpacing":"","marginBottom":"","className":""},"children":[]}]
